source_old_req: The systemHM_process component shall establish environmental assumptions and treat the alarm events arlarm_acc_in and arlarm_adirup_in as legitimate error event entities. It shall ensure that the temporal response of all alarm channels satisfies the zero-delay deadline assumption (Compute_Deadline => 0 ms). The component shall provide safety guarantees by ensuring accurate dispatch of mitigation and recovery commands to the corresponding lower-level fault sources based on real-time aggregated global alarm information. Specifically, the systemHM_process shall map internal closed-loop control logic through structured assignments: incoming alarm events arlarm_acc_in and arlarm_adirup_in shall be processed by the alarmAggregator thread, analyzed by the diagnosisEngine thread, and then passed to the mitigationActor thread to generate system-level hardware reset or software reconfiguration actions, which shall be emitted as systemHM_action_acc_hm and systemHM_action_adirup events.
natural_requirement: The systemHM_process component shall define the implementation-level signal routing for systemHM_process.impl. In the implementation layer, systemHM_process.impl shall route signal flow from arlarm_acc_in< to alarmAggregator.arlarm_acc_in through C1 and signal flow from arlarm_adirup_in< to alarmAggregator.arlarm_adirup_in through C2.

requirement_items.text: The systemHM_process.impl implementation shall route the signal flow from arlarm_acc_in< to alarmAggregator.arlarm_acc_in through connection C1.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The systemHM_process.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from arlarm_acc_in< to alarmAggregator.arlarm_acc_in.

requirement_items.text: The systemHM_process.impl implementation shall route the signal flow from arlarm_adirup_in< to alarmAggregator.arlarm_adirup_in through connection C2.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The systemHM_process.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from arlarm_adirup_in< to alarmAggregator.arlarm_adirup_in.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

natural_requirement_before_behavior_styl: The systemHM_process.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from arlarm_acc_in< to alarmAggregator.arlarm_acc_in. The systemHM_process.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from arlarm_adirup_in< to alarmAggregator.arlarm_adirup_in.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The systemHM_process.impl implementation shall route the signal flow from arlarm_acc_in< to alarmAggregator.arlarm_acc_in through connection C1. The systemHM_process.impl implementation shall route the signal flow from arlarm_adirup_in< to alarmAggregator.arlarm_adirup_in through connection C2.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
